pattern: Two targets fall on the same arithmetic chain exactly when they share a remainder modulo the spacing, so grouping by that remainder reveals which seed destroys the most.
hints: A seed destroys every value congruent to it modulo space, so targets group naturally by their remainder.
Count how many values share each remainder; the largest group is the maximum you can destroy.
Among the remainder groups tied for the largest size, return the smallest original value, since the seed must come from nums.

edge_inputs.description: given example, smallest seed of the best chain
given example where two remainders tie
large space so every value is its own chain
space one collapses everything into one chain
tie broken by smaller value not first occurrence

stress.description: random targets and spacing producing varied remainder group sizes
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, size)
    space = rng.randint(1, 50)
    nums = [rng.randint(1, 200) for _ in range(n)]
    return [nums, space]
stress.note: small range forces many collisions to exercise tie-breaking
